title: Erdős–Gyárfás Conjecture
next_action: Finish the order-22 leg of the E015 bipartite hunt (splits (9,13), (10,12), (11,11); 'python3 bipscan.py run 22' — launched in S016 and left unfinished on a loaded machine, so order 22 is excluded from C034). Any power-free member is an immediate disproof by L035 T3; an empty run extends the parity/mod-4 channel's exclusion to order 22 (23 with a pendant). Then open Thread B3, the one congruence channel L035 does NOT cover: the odd-prime-gcd channel. Concrete first move — decide whether condition (D) (non-terminal degrees >=3) forbids gcd(S) from having an odd prime factor, using Theta(3,3,3) (vertex-taut, non-bipartite, S={3}, internal degrees 2) as the recorded obstruction showing the degree condition and not parity must do the work; look for a mod-p analogue of L035's fan argument (in a 2-connected block, a cycle C and a 2-fan give two through-paths differing by |arc1|-|arc2|, so parity-constancy generalizes to |C| = 2*arc1 mod p) or exhibit a (D)-satisfying gadget with gcd(S) divisible by 3, which would be new disproof material. Deferred alternatives, all live per the standing user instruction: Thread A the pencil endgame (fan case first, A015 cascade pressure points); Thread C the cubic census at order 30; Thread D census mining; Thread E bipartite EGC (now order-bounded: the naive 'C4-free bipartite delta>=3 forces C8' target is false at large order via bipartite double covers of C009's large-girth cubic graphs) and Carr 4/7 -> 1; Thread F 1-atom structure theory and the dedicated {C4,C8}-free generator. NOTE: the E013 order-16 pinched scan launched in S015 is a different agent's job and was left untouched in S016.
